message: 🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Don't throw away the stems! They are completely edible and great for stir-fries or making vegetable broth.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Potato skins contain up to 50% of the nutrients. Give them a good scrub and leave them on!
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Save the root ends! Place them root-down in a small glass of water to regrow them for free.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** If they came with leafy tops, don't bin them! The tops are edible and make a fantastic zero-waste pesto.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Try not to peel them! Over half of an apple's fiber and antioxidants are stored in the skin.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Bananas getting too spotty? Peel, slice, and freeze them for instant smoothie bases or future banana bread.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Zest your citrus before juicing and freeze the zest! You can also boil the leftover peels to make a DIY kitchen cleaner.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Wash berries in a 1-part vinegar to 3-parts water bath, dry thoroughly, and store with a paper towel to double their fridge life!
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Never throw away a hard cheese rind! Drop it into soups or pasta sauces while they simmer for a massive flavor boost.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Bread getting stale? Blitz it in a blender for instant breadcrumbs, or cube it, toss in oil, and bake for homemade croutons.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Cut off the bottom two inches of the celery bunch and place it in a shallow bowl of water. It will regrow a whole new stalk!
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Fresh herbs wilting? Chop them up, put them in an ice cube tray, cover with olive oil, and freeze for instant flavor bombs.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Save your meat bones in a freezer bag. Once full, boil them with leftover vegetable scraps to make free, highly nutritious bone broth!
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Only using half? Store the other half in the fridge with the pit still in it, and squeeze a little lemon juice over the flesh to prevent browning.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Don't bin the seeds! Rinse them, toss with olive oil and salt, and roast them in the oven for a crunchy, zero-waste snack.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Don't wash mushrooms under the tap (they act like sponges and go slimy). Just brush the dirt off with a damp paper towel to keep them fresh longer.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Never keep tomatoes in the fridge! The cold temperature permanently degrades their flavor and texture. Keep them on the counter.
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Are your leafy greens looking limp? Soak them in a bowl of ice water for 15 minutes to crisp them right back up!
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Dairy expiring soon? Pour it into ice cube trays and freeze. You can pop a cube straight into your morning coffee or hot chocolate!
🌱 **Zero-Waste Tip:** Sprouted garlic is still safe to eat, but the green shoot can taste bitter. Just slice the clove in half and pop the green bit out with your knife!
